body.title: ReaderDevice: URI scheme handlers + content-addressed blob cache
body.result: fetch_uri() + BlobCache: https/file/calibre/blob handlers, content-addressed cache, 29 tests green
body.description:
No unified fetch layer exists; https, calibre, and file sources are handled separately in three different places. Build the shared fetch/cache pipeline that all ReaderDevice output modes sit on top of.

Scheme handlers: https:// (urllib, HTML strip, 4000-char trim), calibre://ID (Calibre library lookup via metadata.db → epub/mobi/pdf file path), file:///path (direct read), blob://sha256:hex (cache hit — skip fetch). comms:// deferred to v2.

Blob cache: content-addressed store at ~/.unseen_university/blobs/<sha256[:2]>/<sha256>.blob.json (metadata) + .blob.bin (raw bytes). Cache hit on sha256 match — idempotent, safe for multi-instance concurrency. No DB required.

**Affected files:** devices/reader/__init__.py (new), devices/reader/uri.py (new — scheme dispatch + handlers), devices/reader/blob_cache.py (new — content-addressed cache)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; no Postgres needed (pure filesystem + hash); follow BaseDevice pattern for future shell integration
**Scope boundary:** fetch + cache only; no chunking, no output modes; SummarizerDevice and Igor's reading_tool.py untouched
**Test plan:** pytest — mocked https fetch → blob created with expected sha256; local file:// → correct content cached; cache hit (second call same URI) → no re-fetch; calibre:// mocked via synthetic metadata.db fixture
